scenario.summary: Orchestrating November close — Journal Entry, Anomaly Detection, Reporting
scenario.situation: November month-end close, day 1 morning. The Controller launches the close process. Meridian's close typically takes 6 business days across 6 entities — the target is 4 days. The supervisor coordinates three specialist agents in sequence, handling dependencies and escalating blockers. Last quarter, a missed intercompany elimination delayed the close by 1 day.
agentSteps: Step 1 — Journal Entry & Reconciliation runs first: processes all 1,200 entries, reconciles sub-ledgers, generates intercompany eliminations. Outputs: reconciled trial balances for all 6 entities
Gate check: Supervisor validates that all entities have reconciled trial balances. JE-9005 (JPY 12M FX entry) is above threshold — supervisor routes to Controller for approval

Step 2 — Anomaly Detection scans the reconciled entries: finds manual JE-9010 ($1.2M, no docs) and escalates. Controller reviews, confirms it's a valid accrual, attaches documentation. Supervisor clears the block
Step 3 — Financial Reporting generates the close package once steps 1 and 2 are clear: pulls the reconciled, validated data and produces the full reporting package
Close status dashboard: Supervisor maintains a real-time view — entity-by-entity progress, open items, blockers, estimated completion time
scenario.expectedOutput: Close completed in 4 business days (vs. 6 typical). All entities reconciled, anomalies reviewed, reporting package delivered on day 4. Controller intervened on 2 items (JE-9005 and JE-9010) — everything else was touchless.

stats.label: Enhancement in touchless continuous close by 2027
Of CFOs plan to outsource financial close optimization
